content:
Another important symbol of Phoenix’s sacrifice is the money she receives from the attendant. Despite her own struggles, Phoenix chooses to use the money she received not for herself, but to buy a gift for her grandson. After receiving the money, she immediately goes, “This is what come to me to do,” she said. “I going to the store and buy my child a little windmill they sells, made out of paper. He going to find it hard to believe there such a thing in the world. I’ll march myself back where he waiting, holding it straight up in this hand”. This gesture highlights her selflessness prioritizing her grandson’s happiness over her own needs. This act of giving also reinforces her perseverance, as she continues to push through the difficult journey for the well-being of her loved one.

Ultimately, Phoenix represents enduring love, selflessness, and the willingness to face hardship for the sake of others, making her a powerful symbol of perseverance in the face of life’s many obstacles.


from Week 5 Discussion
